projects.title: Siemens Energy Thesis - High-Temperature Reducer CFD/CHT and Test-Rig Validation
projects.summary: Seven months embedded at Siemens Energy Finspång conducting compressible CFD/CHT, high-temperature instrumentation and root-cause analysis for the Pulsatorn dynamic pressure sensor calibration rig.
highlights: Published thesis: Numerical Investigation of Steady-State Thermo-Fluid Performance of a Reducer for a High-Temperature Dynamic Pressure Sensor Calibration Rig, TRITA-ITM-EX 2026:14.
Compared a legacy 35.05 mm -> 11.25 mm -> 6.0 mm stepped reducer with a 150 mm quintic C2 contraction using adiabatic and CHT simulation campaigns.
Applied Biot number analysis (Bi = 0.003-0.004) and insulation-reference cases to separate internal convective behaviour from external surface-area heat-loss effects.
Commissioned NI-DAQ measurement chains, modified LabVIEW VIs, ran thermal test work up to 700°C and completed formal root cause analysis after heater failure.

projects.title: Structural FEA Reactor Internals Pilot Study
projects.summary: Westinghouse-role-fit ANSYS Mechanical pilot study for a simplified reactor-internals-inspired 316 stainless-steel support plate, covering structural screening, mesh convergence, reaction checks, mechanical load cases, LC4R support mapping and thermal-expansion boundary-condition sensitivity.
highlights: Built a simplified, non-proprietary reactor-internals-inspired support plate in SpaceClaim and screened it in ANSYS Mechanical Student.
Reported the final fine mesh at 245,658 elements and 416,866 nodes after LC1 convergence checks.
Mechanical load cases LC1-LC3 remained below 20.65 MPa peak von Mises, with reaction-force checks matching the applied load resultants.
LC3 reaction-force check gave 3605.6 N in ANSYS versus sqrt(3000^2 + 2000^2) = 3605.5 N from the applied load vector.

Compared constrained thermal expansion against a relaxed 3-2-1 support case; final LC4R documentation records RD1/RD2/RD3 constraints, negligible remote reactions and rejected pivot/missing-result solve states.
Positioned honestly as standards-readiness evidence: not an ASME BPVC, Eurocode, EN13001 or certified nuclear-component calculation.

projects.title: Exploratory PyPSA-NL Grid Congestion and BESS Learning Project
projects.summary: Exploratory Netherlands-inspired constrained-grid learning project comparing renewable growth, bottlenecks, connection-capacity limits, BESS siting, flexible connection contracts and targeted reinforcement.
highlights: Built as an exploratory learning project to understand PyPSA/Linopy/HiGHS, grid congestion and flexible connection concepts; retained as supporting evidence, not as a primary project track.

Executive report identified High Wind Offshore Growth as the top-ranked scenario with grid-value score 60.8, adding 201,561 MWh renewable dispatch and reducing backup generation by the same amount versus base.
Best BESS sweep option was Groningen 400 MW / 4 h, with 8/8 validation checks passed and a dashboard/reporting layer for decision support.

projects.title: Alleima Industrial Energy Performance Mapping
projects.summary: Industrial energy performance methodology covering KPI/EnPI design, metering readiness, load-driver logic, deviation detection and regulatory comparison for decarbonization decisions.
highlights: Developed and independently validated a quantitative methodology for industrial energy performance mapping rather than a one-off audit checklist.
Mapped load-driver logic, metering gaps and deviation-detection needs from noisy operational data into documented technical reporting.
Compared regulatory and energy-audit requirements across seven jurisdictions, with electrical utilities and compressed air systems treated as relevant electrification pathways.

projects.title: Hylkysaari Smart Energy Island Modelling
projects.summary: IDA ICE, HOMER Pro and Excel modelling for Valo Finland's Hylkysaari smart sustainable tourism concept, comparing energy demand reduction, renewable supply and waste-heat recovery pathways.
highlights: Compared BAU and two improved scenarios using three KPIs: nearly zero energy demand, nearly total renewable energy supply and nearly full waste-heat recovery.
Report conclusion favoured combining scenario 1 solar PV with scenario 2 insulation and heat recovery; solar collectors were not attractive because discounted payback approached project lifetime.
Contributed to the business model, IDA ICE modelling and report revision for a real-time guest-facing energy dashboard concept.

projects.title: Residential Heating Systems Techno-Economic Comparison
projects.summary: Comparative assessment of district heating, solar PV with heat pump, solar PV with electric boiler, and solar thermal with thermal storage for residential heat supply.
highlights: Built CAPEX/OPEX/LCOE/payback interpretation around hourly demand and irradiance data for residential heat alternatives.
Compared direct electrification and district-heating alternatives with attention to solar collector calculations and report traceability.
Evidence source includes KTH group report contribution in data collection, solar collector calculations and technical writing.

projects.title: Thermal Energy Storage for District Cooling Peak Shaving
projects.summary: Thermal energy storage concept work for peak shaving and district cooling, comparing phase-change and ice-storage options with control and pricing logic.
highlights: Structured the project around electricity price signals, storage control strategies and technology metrics for shifting thermal loads.
Compared ice and PCM storage options on efficiency, volume, cost and controllability trade-offs.
Useful ABB-facing evidence because it links thermal heating/cooling, optimisation logic and techno-economic framing.

projects.title: Germany Energy-Environment-Economy Transition Analysis
projects.summary: Structured analysis of Germany's energy transition pathways using LEAP modelling tool, evaluating electricity cost and carbon implications across technology scenarios.
highlights: Used LEAP to analyse electricity, cost and carbon-emissions implications for Germany's energy transition pathways.
Project scope included demand projections, technology and fuel assumptions, NDC 2030 comparison, emissions and social cost of technology systems.
Contributed Sankey diagram, SDG 7.1 / energy poverty material, sustainable-development perspective and supporting figures.

projects.title: District Heating Dispatch Optimisation
projects.summary: Multi-asset heat dispatch optimisation with CHP, heat pumps, biomass/geothermal assets, outages, CO2 and electricity-price coupling.
highlights: Designed and implemented a multi-objective MILP dispatch model from first principles using Python/PuLP.
Coupled electricity market price signals to heat-source dispatch across CHP, heat pump, geothermal and storage assets.
Compared cost and emissions trade-offs across base, future-demand, storage, cost-minimisation and emissions-minimisation scenarios.

projects.title: Distribution Grid Study
projects.summary: CIGRE MV network-style power-flow, N-1 and 24-hour simulation study with EV charging, PV and storage impacts.
highlights: Screened line loading, voltage behaviour and contingency sensitivity.
Compared EV, PV and storage assumptions across time-varying demand.

projects.title: Heating Demand Forecasting
projects.summary: Day-ahead building heating-demand forecasting using weather and temporal features, baseline models and neural networks.
highlights: Forecasted day-ahead heating need for a university building using weather and temporal features including outdoor temperature, wind speed and global radiation.
Compared logistic regression, shallow neural network and deep neural network models with test accuracies of 92.03%, 93.39% and 93.72%.
Selected the shallow neural network as the better balance between accuracy, complexity and overfitting risk for smart-building and district-heating use cases.

projects.title: PyNEXUS Green Hydrogen Network Model
projects.summary: Coupled offshore wind, PEM electrolyzer and hydrogen-pipeline model with MILP dispatch optimisation.
highlights: Connects portfolio hydrogen-network modelling with KTH Power-to-X coursework on electrolysis, hydrogen derivatives and renewable surplus conversion.
Models wind-to-hydrogen conversion, pipeline delivery and curtailment KPIs.
Compares cost and emissions objectives with HiGHS/Pyomo optimisation.

projects.title: EU ETS Exposure Calculator
projects.summary: Excel and Streamlit toolkit for estimating Scope 1 emissions, EUA cost exposure and carbon-intensity trends.
highlights: Calculates ETS scenario summaries and EUA price sensitivity.
Includes workbook parsing, CLI runner, PDF export and regression tests.

projects.title: Industrial Energy KPI Toolkit
projects.summary: Python, Excel and Streamlit toolkit for industrial EnPIs, baseline normalisation, anomaly flags and management reporting.
highlights: Built reproducible engineering decision-support tooling with CLI interfaces, automated tests, plots and PDF reports.
Generates KPI results, action logs, baseline-normalised EnPIs and anomaly flags for ISO 50001-style management review.
Designed for engineering decision support rather than one-off data exploration.

projects.title: Rotating Machinery Vibration Mini-Lab
projects.summary: Low-cost vibration workflow using phone accelerometer data, filtering, FFT/PSD, spectrograms and report generation.
highlights: Compares operating points through frequency fingerprints and RMS metrics.
Packages outputs as HTML reports, plots and summary CSVs.

projects.title: Built Environment Energy Simulation - IDA ICE Supply System Comparison
projects.summary: IDA ICE building energy simulation comparing supply-system alternatives, demand behaviour and performance implications for a built-environment assignment.
highlights: Compared supply-system options through building energy simulation outputs.
Translated assignment results into energy-performance interpretation and design implications.

projects.title: Emergency Energy Module - Post-Disaster Polygeneration, Philippines
projects.summary: Post-disaster polygeneration concept for emergency electricity, cooling and water support in a Philippines context.
highlights: Mapped emergency loads and modular energy-system functions.
Structured the concept around resilient post-disaster service delivery.

projects.title: M-TES PCM Thermal Lab - RT60 HC Discharge Characterisation
projects.summary: Latent-heat mobile thermal energy storage lab characterising RT60 HC discharge behaviour, measurement interpretation and thermal-response trends.
highlights: Evaluated a latent heat-based M-TES prototype with oil bath, water cooling, coil heat exchanger, HTF reservoir, pump and data-logging chain.
Worked with ten PT100 sensors, a type-T thermocouple, Omega flow meter and Keithley DAQ-connected measurement workflow.
Connected discharge behaviour, PCM properties and thermal-response trends to storage performance limits.

projects.title: Battery Cell Discharge Performance Testing - Ni-MH, Li-Po and Lead-Acid
projects.summary: Battery discharge lab comparing Ni-MH, Li-Po and lead-acid cells through C-rate behaviour, voltage curves and performance interpretation.
highlights: Compared Ni-MH, Li-Po and lead-acid batteries through C-rate, temperature and chemistry-specific discharge tests.
Test matrix included Ni-MH at 1C, 0.5C and 2C, plus temperature cases at +40°C and -10°C.
Interpreted empirical voltage/capacity behaviour against redox operation, degradation factors and recommended limits.

projects.title: Waste-to-Energy Proposals - Delhi and Kozhikode
projects.summary: Waste-to-energy assignment work comparing municipal solid waste handling, segregation and energy-recovery concepts for Kozhikode and Delhi.
highlights: Kozhikode concept mapped a 600 tonne/day waste stream with biological and thermal processing pathways for electricity, heat and public-transport links.
Delhi proposal framed landfill pressure, poor segregation and open landfill burning as the system problem before proposing WtE containment and processing.
Compared city-specific constraints in collection, sorting, environmental impact and energy recovery.

projects.title: Numerical Heat Transfer - Finite Difference, Time-Stepping and Advection Schemes
projects.summary: Python assignments covering catastrophic cancellation, numerical differentiation, Laplace stencils, Euler vs RK4 and advection scheme comparison.
highlights: Analysed catastrophic cancellation, machine precision and numerical differentiation error, including forward and central differencing of cos(x).
Solved 2D heat-conduction problems using finite-difference stencils and compared direct and time-marching convergence behaviour.
Compared Euler and RK4 time stepping for the 2D heat equation and Lax-Friedrichs versus Lax-Wendroff advection behaviour.

projects.title: GridFlex - ML-Driven Energy Storage Optimisation Concept
projects.summary: Entrepreneurship assignment translating renewable-grid storage optimisation, real-time data and machine learning into a product concept and business model.
highlights: Developed GridFlex as a service for dynamically managing energy storage in renewable-powered solar and wind grids.
Concept uses machine learning and real-time data analysis to balance storage output against grid requirements and renewable availability.
Outlined prototype testing with simulated data, customer interviews and predictive weather-aware storage adjustment.

projects.title: Techno-Economic Analysis and Development of Hylkysaari Island for Smart and Sustainable Tourism
projects.summary: Techno-economic and energy-system study for transforming Hylkysaari Island into a smart, sustainable tourism destination by 2030, comparing BAU, Golden Marguerite and Fireweed scenarios with emphasis on near-zero energy demand, local renewables and waste heat recovery.
highlights: IDA ICE and HOMER Pro scenario modelling for a smart-energy island concept.
Framed tourism energy demand through nearly zero energy demand, renewable supply and waste-heat recovery KPIs.

projects.title: Techno-economic comparative assessment of residential heating solutions: PV/direct electrification vs district heating
projects.summary: Assessment for BRF Arstaterrassen comparing solar thermal with thermal storage, solar PV with electric boilers and solar PV with heat pumps as seasonal alternatives to district heating.
highlights: Compared solar thermal storage, PV-electric boiler and PV-heat-pump alternatives against district heating.
Used techno-economic metrics to discuss seasonal heating alternatives for a residential building context.

projects.title: Energy-Environment-Economy analysis of Germany
projects.summary: Energy-sector analysis focused on Germany's greenhouse-neutrality pathway to 2045, including electricity, cost, emissions, renewable integration, storage and nuclear reintroduction scenarios.
highlights: LEAP-based national energy scenario modelling for Germany's 2045 greenhouse-neutrality pathway.
Compared electricity mix, cost, emissions, renewable integration, storage and nuclear reintroduction scenarios.

projects.title: Bicycle Design Competition (SAE INDIA)
projects.summary: Project team lead for Team TRAMS in Bicycle Design Competition 2020, coordinating design, analysis, documentation and competition deliverables.
highlights: Led Team TRAMS for SAEINDIA Bicycle Design Competition 2020 and coordinated design-report deliverables.
Documented AISI 4130 chromoly frame design, static CAE checks and manufacturing/cost rationale.

projects.title: Automatic Sanitizer Dispenser
projects.summary: Automated hand sanitizer dispenser built by a mechanical engineering student team using e-waste and basic Arduino programming.
highlights: Rapid hardware prototype for contactless dispensing using basic Arduino logic and reused components.
Kept as early evidence of practical build-test iteration and low-cost prototyping.

projects.title: Wireless Charging Technology and Application in Automobile Industry
projects.summary: Study of wireless charging solutions for portable and automotive environments.
highlights: Automotive technology study covering wireless charging concepts, deployment routes and technical constraints.
Useful as early background for electrified transport and charging-infrastructure thinking.

projects.title: Peltier Refrigerator
projects.summary: Compressor-free refrigeration concept based on Peltier cooling with no rotary power-consuming component.
highlights: Bachelor design project on a thermoelectric refrigerator using the Peltier effect as a compact compressor-free cooling concept.
Useful as early evidence of thermal-hardware prototyping interest before later KTH heat-transfer and Siemens test-rig work.

projects.title: Tractor Design Competition 2019
projects.summary: Team member of Trac Toro Racing for SAEINDIA Tractor Design Competition 2019, with design-review evidence across chassis sizing, braking, steering, engine/transmission and QFD-style design priorities.
highlights: SAEINDIA tractor design report with chassis, braking, steering, engine/transmission and QFD-style design-review evidence.
Documented AISI 1018 frame sizing, safety-factor reasoning, 25 km/h braking assumptions and vehicle-system packaging constraints.

projects.title: BAJA SAE 2019
projects.summary: Engine and transmission team member for BAJA SAEINDIA 2019.
highlights: Engine and transmission team role in an SAEINDIA off-road vehicle competition context.
Kept as early vehicle-systems evidence rather than a current target-role headline.

projects.title: B.Tech Final Year Interactive Robot Project
projects.associatedWith: College of Engineering Perumon / APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University
projects.summary: Final-year interactive robot project combining IRJET control-system/subsystem integration work with the published frame and locomotion design: Raspberry Pi/cloud control, surveillance, SOC indication, steering integration, drivetrain sizing and ANSYS deformation screening.
highlights: B.Tech final-year project represented by the IRJET control-system/subsystem-integration manuscript and the IRJET frame/locomotion publication.
Connected Raspberry Pi/cloud/voice control, surveillance, SOC indication and steering integration with mechanical drivetrain and frame design evidence.
